FlareSolverr cost-to-serve is well under $0.001/call so the 5cr sticker was almost entirely margin and read expensive next to ScraperAPI ultra_premium ($0.03+) and ScrapeGraphAI's stealth-extract ($0.034 on Pro). New scheme keeps render distinct from full semantically (FlareSolverr-backed; CF JS-challenge handler) but aligned in price; under a `full → render` escalation pattern, average per-fallback cost converges on $0.03. Subscription buckets unchanged — render still draws from the smaller render-bucket (Scraper Pro $25/mo: 15K render calls; Business $50/mo: 50K render calls). Companion work in the work scraper repo at /media/veracrypt2/NodeJS/ScraperService: scrapeWithScraperApi and /raw-fetch migrated from ScraperAPI to LANAgent on 2026-05-05; both now use the same callLANAgentScrape helper with full → render escalation. End-to-end verified in production: [LANAGENT] PASS https://www.foxnews.com/... (tier=full, credits, charged=3). 5cr slot now open for a future premium tier (residential proxies, 4G mobile IPs) without breaking the existing pricing ladder. v2.25.24 base: Per-page audit of every admin body for the inline-style mobile-overflow issue class. One remaining offender: 'Grant credits' admin-actions row on user-detail and wallet-detail pages used .row-flex with inline width:160px and flex:1 declarations that overrode responsive rules. Fixed by making .row-flex wrap at ≤880px with !important overrides forcing its inputs to a full row. All other admin pages audited clean. Promotions and Scrapes pages had inline grid-template-columns declarations that overrode responsive rules — Promotions scrolled horizontally on mobile (1fr 360px sidebar form), Scrapes had Top Services and Top Agents side-by-side on phones (1fr 1fr). Both replaced with new .grid-aside and .grid-2 classes that collapse to 1fr at ≤880px. v2.25.22 base: gateway admin dashboard responsive overhaul. Recent Payments table no longer crammed; dashboard grid switched from grid-3 to dash-grid (Payments 2fr / Agents 1fr / Tickets 1fr on desktop; Payments spans both cols on tablet; all stacked on phone). Long emails truncate with title tooltip. Top nav collapses to hamburger drawer on screens ≤880px (was wrapping 9 links across multiple lines). Wide tables scroll horizontally inside cards. v2.25.21 base: Gateway + BETA agent recovery. Fixed silently-broken admin login (express.urlencoded was missing on the gateway, so the HTML form's email field never reached the handler — every login fell into the anti-enumeration tarpit). Reconnected gateway↔BETA after a 24-day stale-key outage caused by the April 11 mongo ransomware wipe (6,917 401s on /credits/balance, BETA excluded from routing pool). Bootstrapped BETA with $5 of on-chain SKYNET credits (gateway wallet swapped 0.008 BNB → 268,433 SKYNET → BETA → 503 credits credited). Reworked pickBestAgent for strict primary/fallback by reputation: ALICE always wins when online+funded; BETA only sees traffic when ALICE is offline or out of credits. Stood up backup tooling: server-side cron + local pull script in scripts/deployment/backup-beta.sh writing to _BackUps_/. v2.25.20 base: PR review pass on 10 ALICE-generated PRs (#2083-#2092).
milestone: Gateway admin panel works again. BETA is a functional fallback in routing without stealing traffic from the primary. BETA backups run nightly on the server (14d retention) with a one-shot local pull for cold storage (30d retention) — the failure mode where ransomware or operator error wipes BETA's mongo without warning is now mitigated. Pre-April-11 wallet seed remains unrecoverable; current wallet 0x40b03c8b... is the operational one going forward.

Render stays semantically distinct from full (FlareSolverr-backed; handles real CF JS challenges) but aligned in price, so under a `full → render` escalation pattern the average per-fallback cost converges on $0.03 instead of $0.05. adminDashboardAuditPass_2026_05_05.description: Audit of every admin Body() function in admin.mjs for the inline-style mobile-overflow issue class fixed in v2.25.22 / v2.25.23. Per-page results — Dashboard: dash-grid + grid-6 KPIs already responsive (OK). Users / Wallets: grid-4 KPIs + single-col list (OK). User detail / Wallet detail: grid-4 KPIs + admin-action row-flex with inline width:160px / flex:1 inputs (FIXED). Agents: single-col card (OK). Payments: grid-3 KPIs + 7-col table (OK — table scrolls inside card on mobile via existing @media rule). Subscriptions: 6-col table with embedded progress bars (OK). Promotions: grid-aside (fixed in v2.25.23). Tickets: wide table + dialog with internal row-flex (OK — dialog is min(720px,90vw) and row-flex now wraps). Scrapes: grid-2 (fixed in v2.25.23). Audit: single-col + 6-col table (OK). The one remaining issue (.row-flex 'Grant credits' row) was fixed by adding a @media (max-width: 880px) block that makes .row-flex wrap, gives label its own row, and forces inputs/selects/textareas to flex: 1 1 100% !important and width: auto !important. The !important is intentional — it's the only way to override the inline width:160px and flex:1 declarations sitting on the same elements without rewriting markup. No remaining inline grid-template-columns declarations exist anywhere in admin.mjs.

adminDashboardMobileFollowup_2026_05_05.description: Two pages from the v2.25.22 admin overhaul still rendered as wide multi-column layouts on mobile because they used inline grid-template-columns declarations that overrode the responsive rules in SHARED_STYLE. Promotions (/admin/promotions) used inline 'grid-template-columns: 1fr 360px' for its table+sidebar-form layout — the 360px sidebar forced the page wider than any phone viewport, so the whole page (including sticky header) had to scroll horizontally. Scrapes (/admin/scrapes) used inline 'grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr' for Top Services + Top Agents — both charts stayed side-by-side at phone widths. Fix: added .grid-2 (2-col → 1-col below 880px) and .grid-aside (1fr 360px → 1fr below 880px) to SHARED_STYLE; replaced both inline declarations with the new classes. Principle going forward: always stack on mobile unless the components are small (KPIs, pills, etc.). Multi-column layouts in admin.mjs should now use one of .grid-2, .grid-3, .grid-4, .grid-6, .grid-aside, .dash-grid instead of inline grid-template-columns — the class defines both desktop layout and the breakpoint, so the page can never end up wider than the viewport.
files: /opt/api-gateway/admin.mjs (gateway server, untracked) — added .grid-2 and .grid-aside to SHARED_STYLE, swapped inline grid styles in promotionsBody and scrapesBody
adminDashboardMobileFollowup_2026_05_05.verification: Syntax check passes. PM2 restart clean. Health endpoint returns success. .grid-2 and .grid-aside present in SHARED_STYLE; promotionsBody and scrapesBody now use class-based grids with no inline grid-template-columns.

adminDashboardResponsive_2026_05_05.description: Gateway admin dashboard responsive overhaul. Operator reported Recent Payments table was crammed even on desktop (the dashboard used a grid-3 layout, so the four-column payments table had to fit in ~33% page width — long emails like portablediag@protonmail.com shoved the numeric columns into a sliver) and the top nav was unusable on mobile (nine links wrapped onto multiple lines, shoving sign-out off-screen). Fix is almost entirely additive CSS appended to SHARED_STYLE in admin.mjs plus a single nav-toggle button injection in layout(): (1) New dash-grid template — Payments 2fr / Agents 1fr / Tickets 1fr on desktop; Payments spans both cols on tablet ≤980px; all three stacked on phone ≤600px. (2) Email cells gain email-cell class with text-overflow:ellipsis + title= tooltip showing the full address on hover. (3) Hamburger nav-toggle button on screens ≤880px collapses the topnav into a vertical drawer below the header. (4) Wide tables overflow-x:auto inside their cards on narrow screens with momentum touch scrolling. (5) Compact mobile polish — reduced card padding, smaller h1 + KPI value, kv grid drops to single column, toolbar inputs flex to fill, bar-row labels narrow. No markup changes outside layout() and dashboardBody().
files: /opt/api-gateway/admin.mjs (gateway server, untracked) — appended responsive CSS to SHARED_STYLE, added nav-toggle button to layout(), switched dashboardBody grid-3 to dash-grid, marked email cell with email-cell class
adminDashboardResponsive_2026_05_05.verification: Syntax check passes. PM2 restart clean. Health endpoint returns success. /admin/login renders with new CSS classes (6+ matches in served HTML for nav-toggle/topbar). Visual verification at narrow breakpoints requires browser inspection.

gatewayBetaRecovery_2026_05_05.description: Three independent gateway/BETA fixes that compounded into a silently-broken admin panel and a never-routed BETA. (1) Gateway admin login form silently dropped the email field — express.urlencoded was missing so application/x-www-form-urlencoded POSTs to /admin/auth/request arrived with req.body={}, the handler fell into the anti-enumeration tarpit, and the operator received ?sent=1 in the URL but no email. Added express.urlencoded({extended:true,limit:'1mb'}) after the JSON parser. (2) Gateway↔BETA paired with a never-valid API key since the April 11 ransomware wipe; BETA's externalcreditbalances was empty, the stored key authenticated against nothing, and 6,917 calls to /credits/balance failed 401 over 24 days. Inserted the missing externalcreditbalances doc on BETA pairing wallet 0x40b03c8b... with the gateway's existing key. (3) BETA had 0 credits so was excluded from the funded pool; routing logic was weighted-random across funded only, with the consequence that even with credits added BETA at rep=0 vs ALICE at rep=187 only had ~0.5% selection probability. Replaced with strict primary/fallback by reputation — among funded agents, prefer online (lastSeen<10min), sort by successCount-failCount descending, return top. Bootstrapped BETA with $5 of on-chain credits via gateway wallet → PancakeSwap V2 swap → SKYNET transfer → BETA /credits/purchase verification (tx 0xca0942a0...). Assigned BETA agentId 2931 so POST /agents/2931/:service direct-route works. Capability gating remains at the DB query level (services: service filter), so render-tier scrapes still cannot route to BETA — no fallback for render tier when ALICE is down, which is correct (BETA lacks FlareSolverr).
files: /opt/api-gateway/index.mjs (gateway server, untracked) — added express.urlencoded; rewrote pickBestAgent for strict primary/fallback by reputation
gatewayBetaRecovery_2026_05_05.verification: Form-encoded POST to /admin/auth/request → 302 ?sent=1 → magic link issued → email sent. Gateway → BETA /credits/balance returns 200 {success:true,credits:503}. 401 spam stopped. pickBestAgent simulation: ALICE picked when both online; BETA picks up when ALICE simulated offline for plugin-websearch/news/scraper/youtube-download. Direct route GET /agents/2931 returns BETA identity.

betaBackupTooling_2026_05_05.description: Two-layer backup setup for BETA: (1) Server-side cron on BETA itself — /root/backup-beta.sh + cron '15 3 * * *', daily mongodump --gzip --archive to /root/lanagent-backups/beta-YYYY-MM-DD.archive.gz with 14-day retention. Restore via docker exec -i BETA-mongodb mongorestore --gzip --archive --drop. (2) Local pull script for cold storage — scripts/deployment/backup-beta.sh runs from the dev machine, streams a fresh mongodump over SSH (no remote temp file), rsyncs config (.env, docker-compose.yml, CLAUDE.local.md, ecosystem.config.cjs, package.json) and data dirs (data/, workspace/, quarantine/ — excludes logs/ and node_modules/), writes a manifest with git rev / container statuses / mongo db sizes / wallet addresses, and seals everything into /media/veracrypt1/NodeJS/_BackUps_/beta-backup-<UTC-stamp>.tar.gz with 30-day retention. First run: 14M archive. Safe to run while BETA is live. Closes the post-April-11 backup gap where /root/lanagent-backups/ existed but was empty.
betaBackupTooling_2026_05_05.verification: Server-side test run produced 9.0M archive at /root/lanagent-backups/beta-2026-05-05.archive.gz. Local pull test run produced 14M archive at _BackUps_/beta-backup-2026-05-05T17-14-15Z.tar.gz with all expected components and a clean manifest. Backups not yet round-tripped via mongorestore — flagged as a follow-up.

skynetBotBYOK_2026_05_04.description: Per-user gateway API keys for the SKYNET API Telegram bot. BotUser schema gains AES-256-GCM encrypted apiKey blob (sharing WALLET_ENCRYPTION_KEY with the wallet service) plus plaintext last-4 hint for identification without decryption. New src/services/userKey.js (set/get/clear/validate/redact + KEY_REGEX). Gateway service refactored: callService/socialDownload/getCreditsBalance accept opts.apiKey override; singleton key is now the bot's fallback only. New centralized src/services/access.js checkPaidAccess returning {allowed, apiKey} replaces 7 duplicated local copies across command files. ~30 callsites threaded with the user's key when present. incrementUsage gated on !access.apiKey so BYOK calls don't tick free-tier counters. Three new commands in meta.js: /setkey (validates against gateway → deletes source message → stores), /mykey (shows last 4 + load date + live balance), /clearkey. autoDetect handler captures gsk_-pattern keys pasted into chat and runs the same flow without requiring the /setkey verb. Verified: format validation, gateway validation against real/fake keys, encrypt/decrypt roundtrip, all four checkPaidAccess scenarios (free under/over limit, BYOK under/over limit).

rumble403Fix_2026_05_04.description: Pinned curl_cffi --impersonate target from bare 'chrome' (which picks chrome-99) to 'chrome-131' for both Cloudflare-bypass and stored-cookie paths in src/api/plugins/ytdlp.js. Bare 'chrome' default's JA3/JA4 fingerprint diverged from FlareSolverr's Chrome 142, so Cloudflare rejected the cf_clearance cookie despite the cookie being valid. Empirical sweep across yt-dlp's available impersonate targets: chrome-99/110/116/133 all 403 against Rumble; chrome-124/131 succeed. Also extended src/core/aiIntentDetector.js downloadVideo (intent 64) and downloadAudio (intent 166) with 30 platform-named training examples covering tiktok/rumble/bitchute/streamable/dailymotion/x.com/twitch/bilibili/soundcloud — vector store auto-rebuilds via indexAllIntents on next agent restart. Verified: Rumble (Bannon_s_Warroom_Is_Targeting_Specific_RINOs_to_Oppose_in_Primaries.mp4, 7.1 MB) and BitChute (Nima_R._Alkhorshid…, 259 MB) both download cleanly via ALICE's /api/command/execute endpoint with natural-language phrasings.

socialDownloadPhase23_2026_05_04.description: Phase 2: Cloudflare-bypass for Rumble, BitChute, fb.watch via long-lived FlareSolverr sessions (fsEnsureSession in src/utils/flareSolverr.js) — sessions persist cf_clearance across requests so Cloudflare doesn't escalate challenge difficulty into FS timeouts on repeated solves. yt-dlp invocation combines --cookies (Netscape file written from FS solution.cookies) + --user-agent (FS-issued UA) + --impersonate chrome. The TLS fingerprint match is required because Cloudflare's cf_clearance is bound to the TLS handshake of the client that solved the challenge — without --impersonate, the cookie is rejected. Phase 3: Cookie-required sites (instagram.com, facebook.com, fb.watch) read from a stored Netscape file at $LANAGENT_COOKIES_DIR/<host>.txt (default ~/.config/lanagent/cookies/) outside the deploy dir so cookies survive deployments. New JWT-protected admin endpoints at /api/admin/cookies on the agent (GET list / POST :host / DELETE :host) with allow-list of supported hosts and Netscape-format validation. SKYNET API Telegram bot auto-downloads supported URLs (tiktok/rumble/bitchute/streamable/dailymotion/bilibili/twitch) when posted to chat, plus a new /dl <url> [mp3|mp4] manual command. Verified: BitChute mp4 download (Who_The_Fed_Actually_Serves.mp4, 2.22 MB) end-to-end; Rumble cookies+impersonate pipeline validated by sustained HLS streaming through the CF wall; admin-endpoint round trip tested; yt-dlp confirmed to consume stored cookies file via --cookies flag.

socialDownloadPhase1_2026_05_04.description: Generic /social/download route on agent (POST /api/external/social/download) and gateway (POST /social/download for MP4 at 10 credits, POST /social/audio for MP3 at 8 credits). Agent-side dispatcher: x.com / twitter.com / *.twitter.com → twitter plugin's bespoke download action; everything else → yt-dlp's ~1800-site extractor list. Returns the same downloadUrl-token shape as /youtube/download so existing consumers only need to swap the path. ytdlp plugin _buildBaseCommand now adds --impersonate chrome (via curl-cffi) for hosts that block default HTTP fingerprints — TikTok, Instagram, Facebook — without affecting YouTube/SoundCloud paths. Companion POST /api/external/social/probe (free, no credit charge) returns which extractor would handle a given URL plus extracted metadata. Verified end-to-end: YouTube mp3, SoundCloud mp3, TikTok mp4 (1.91 MB), Streamable mp4 (2.90 MB), Dailymotion mp4 (8.53 MB), Bilibili (probe), plus gateway-level Streamable /social/download and SoundCloud /social/audio with correct credit debits (10 and 8 respectively).
socialDownloadPhase1_2026_05_04.phase2: Cloudflare-gated sites (Rumble, BitChute) — deferred until Phase 1 has soaked. Plan is to reuse the existing FlareSolverr infrastructure used by the scraper plugin.
socialDownloadPhase1_2026_05_04.phase3: Cookie-required sites (Instagram videos, Facebook, X.com video tweets that aren't public-API-accessible). Deferred until Phase 2 ships. Plan is a cookie-jar pipeline managed via the admin UI.

gatewayAdminConsole2026_05_04.description: Magic-link admin UI at /admin on the gateway VPS. Allow-listed to ADMIN_EMAIL (default portablediag@protonmail.com); link expires in 15 min and is single-use; non-admin email lookups get an identical 200 response after a 250-450ms tarpit so timing/output can't enumerate which address is allow-listed. Sessions are short-lived JWTs in HttpOnly + SameSite=Lax + Secure cookies scoped to /admin. Existing X-Admin-Key header auth preserved alongside cookie auth on every /admin/api/* endpoint so curl scripts keep working. 9 nav sections (Dashboard, Users, Wallets, Agents, Payments, Subscriptions, Promotions, Tickets, Scrapes, Audit) covering every entity in the gateway DB. Wallet-bootstrapped accounts (separate creditbalances collection that authenticates via wallet signature instead of email/password — Mindswarm and similar) had no admin view at all before this and now have parallel coverage to email-signup PortalUsers, with a type classifier (eth wallet vs portal-bridge stub vs test record) and BscScan tx links on the detail page. Every admin write action (grant credits, revoke key, mark verified, agent toggle/delete, promotion CRUD, ticket status) is recorded to a new AdminAudit collection visible at /admin/audit.

portalMagicLinkAuth2026_05_04.description: Passwordless sign-up and sign-in on the public portal. Single endpoint POST /portal/magic-link covers both flows: existing user → sign-in link; new email → account auto-created (passwordless stub bcrypt hash, default gsk_* API key, welcome email) and a 'finish setting up' link is sent. GET /portal/magic-verify?t=<token> consumes the 15-min single-use token, marks emailVerified=true, sets the JWT, and redirects to the dashboard. Auth modal updated: toggle link now correctly flips between 'Already have an account? Log in' / 'Don't have an account? Sign up' depending on mode (was showing the wrong text in login mode); magic-link button reads 'Email me a sign-up link' or 'Email me a sign-in link' depending on mode. Sits alongside the existing email+password and BSC-wallet signup paths.

supportTicketPersistence2026_05_04.description: support-poller.mjs now writes each new email arriving at support@lanagent.net to a supporttickets Mongo collection (idempotent on Message-ID), in addition to the existing instant Telegram notification. Admin UI's Tickets page reads from this collection: triage list filtered by status (open/pending/closed), modal for each ticket showing the body + ctxSnapshot, ability to add notes and change status. Sets up the data foundation for v3 LLM-drafted replies described in docs/proposals/ai-support-system.md (still recommended to sit on v2 for 2+ weeks before building v3).

alicePrReviewPass2026_05_03.description: Reviewed 18 ALICE-generated PRs (#2065-#2082) one by one. Outcome: 1 merged, 1 salvaged, 16 closed each with an explanatory comment. Merged: #2065 (ipstack getTimezones batch — mirrors fetchGeolocationBatch, reuses fetchTimezone + getCachedData helpers, no new deps). Salvaged: #2067 (repoInfo.js — silent } catch {} → logger.debug for git-remote fallback observability; rest of PR closed because making getOriginRepo async would break in-file sync callers, retryOperation around execSync is pointless, and unused NodeCache import bloats the file). 16 closures categorized by root cause (breaking changes, would-crash-on-import code, stubs returning success:true, financial/safety hazards from blanket retry on writes, misused utilities, redundant duplicates of existing code, description-vs-diff mismatches, operational-doc loss). All comments cite specific file:line locations and explain the underlying issue.

gatewayUsageThresholdEmails2026_05_03.description: Transactional emails fire automatically as a portal user crosses 80%/90%/100% of their subscription bucket (basic + render, separately) or their credit-pool balance. Credit thresholds are computed against creditAlertBaseline (snapshot of balance after most recent top-up) so each top-up resets the alert window — avoids the 'lifetime % spent' problem where a heavy user is at 99% forever. Sub thresholds reset on invoice.paid (billing_reason: subscription_cycle). Fire-once-per-cycle enforced via atomic findOneAndUpdate on per-threshold notifications.{credits,subBasic,subRender}{80,90,100} timestamps so concurrent debits can't double-send. Send is fire-and-forget — /scrape never blocks on SMTP. End-to-end verified: 6 emails fired correctly, idempotency verified (no resend when flag already set).

gatewayPasswordResetAndVerification2026_.description: Full account-management email suite. New routes: POST /portal/forgot-password (always returns 200 to avoid leaking which addresses are registered), POST /portal/reset-password (consumes single-use token + sets new password), GET /portal/reset?token=… (HTML form linked from reset email), GET /portal/verify?token=… (one-shot verification page that flips emailVerified=true), POST /portal/resend-verification. Hooks into existing routes: /portal/signup fires welcome + email-verification on completion; /portal/api-keys POST/DELETE/regenerate fires create/revoke notifications with name/prefix/source-IP/timestamp; /portal/cancel-subscription fires cancellation confirmation with period-end date. New PortalToken collection with TTL index — Mongo auto-removes expired tokens. Tokens are SHA-256 hashed in DB; raw value only ever leaves in the email URL. Minting a fresh token invalidates prior unused tokens of the same purpose so stale links can't be replayed.

gatewaySupportInbox2026_05_03.description: support@lanagent.net mailbox provisioned (500MB quota) and monitored by a new support-poller PM2 service on the gateway VPS. Polls IMAPS every 60s for UNSEEN messages. v1: notification-only — Telegram alert with from/subject/body, skips bouncebacks/auto-replies/noreply senders. v2: same flow plus user-context enrichment — looks up the From: address in PortalUser, builds 7-day usage context (sub state with bucket %, credits, recent request totals, top failing routes, most recent failure with error message + age, last payment), and includes that in the Telegram message. Both matched-sender and unmatched-sender paths verified end-to-end. Marks messages \Seen after notify so each ticket pings exactly once. Roadmap to v3 (LLM-drafted replies + Telegram approve flow), v4 (auto-send for trusted categories), v5 (proactive outreach on internal events) documented in docs/proposals/ai-support-system.md.

scrapeVpnRotation2026_05_03.description: Auto-rotate the agent's ExpressVPN exit through a curated 10-location pool when a paid-scrape returns a block-shaped failure (403/406/429/503/Cloudflare challenge/access-denied/rate-limit signature). Up to MAX_VPN_ROTATIONS=2 retries per scrape; cache invalidated on each retry. Surfaces vpnRotation: { location, rotations } in the response on recovery. Skipped on tier='basic' because cheerio fetches are too cheap/fast to justify the 3-5s VPN reconnect cost. Test hook _testBlock: 'once' | 'always' in request body forces simulated blocks for E2E verification without needing a real blocked target. Verified end-to-end: simulated block on tier=stealth rotated smart->usnj and recovered on attempt 1; _testBlock=always exhausted both rotations (smart->usnj->usla) and refunded credits.

gatewayScraperSubscriptions2026_05_03.description: Three monthly Stripe subscription tiers on api.lanagent.net for /scrape and /scrape/batch routes. Two independent quota buckets per plan (basic + render); tier mapping basic/stealth/full->basic counter, render->render counter. Subscriptions are additive to existing one-time credit packages and BNB/SKYNET crypto purchases. When sub bucket exhausted, /scrape falls back to credit pool. Quotas reset on invoice.paid webhook (subscription_cycle). Atomic mongo $expr ops prevent race conditions at the DB level.

subscriptionPromoCoupon2026_05_03.description: When an active Promotion document exists, /portal/subscribe attaches a Stripe coupon (idempotently created via getOrCreateStripeCoupon(percentOff) with deterministic ID lanagent_promo_${pct}pct_once and duration: 'once') to the Checkout session. Discount applies to first invoice only; renewals revert to base price. Existing Launch Special (50%) promo now reduces first month of any sub from $10/$25/$50 to $5/$12.50/$25.

stripeCustomerRecovery2026_05_03.description: getOrCreateStripeCustomer(user) helper validates a stored stripeCustomerId against Stripe before use. If missing (typically left over from test mode after switching to live keys, or deleted in the dashboard), a fresh customer is created and the user record is updated. Used by both /portal/checkout and /portal/subscribe. Reproduced the bug by planting a fake customer ID on a test user, confirmed the helper detects the stale ID and self-heals.

portalPriceLabelFix2026_05_03.description: Three subscription-eligible service cards on api.lanagent.net were showing prices that didn't match what the gateway charged. prReviewBatch2026_05_01.description: Reviewed 8 AI-generated PRs (#2057-#2064). 4 features manually implemented with corrections; 4 closed as broken/dead/speculative. All PRs left with detailed close comments explaining what was wrong and what was implemented (or why nothing was).

watchlistV2LiquidityFix2026_05_01.description: CryptoStrategyAgent.watchlistPriceFetcher set hasLiquidity=true only when V3 or V4 quote >= V2 quote. For V2-only tokens (every small-cap on PancakeSwap, including the SKYNET system token), V3/V4 returned null and hasLiquidity stayed false — the evaluator then counted that as a failure and incremented _failCount on every cycle. SKYNET (our own token) was at _failCount: 16 with system:true preventing removal. Verified on-chain: SKYNET V2 pool 0xf3def...8694a has 37.5M SKYNET / 1.10 BNB reserves (~$1,369 TVL, $0.0000182/token); no V3 pool exists at any fee tier (100/500/3000/10000). Fix: hasLiquidity = any of V2/V3/V4 has amountOut > 0; liquidityDepth = max(out * price) across protocols. Applied to both watchlistPriceFetcher definitions in CryptoStrategyAgent (auto-config branch and active-strategy branch). Reset SKYNET's persisted _failCount to 0 in subagents.state.domainState.strategyRegistry (both base trader and instance copy).

v2154FeeToStakingFlywheel.description: Registry fee income automatically funds staking reward epochs. Tracks self vs external reporter income. registryFees ledger category isolates fee income from LP/treasury/reserve. Routes to staking when epoch ended + fee balance >= threshold (default 500K). Configurable threshold, routing percent (default 100%), epoch duration (default 7 days). Uses scan-friendly RPCs (BlastAPI, dRPC, nodies) with chunked queries.
